sweep.rule: A fact is stated only if >=2 independent sources agree. Pending/forward events are kept in the future tense. Every datapoint is tagged sourced or estimated.
sweep.note: Earnings dates and the macro backdrop are sourced from the 16-Jun-2026 sweep. Implied moves are options-desk estimates (labelled estimated); consensus figures are flagged where not dual-confirmed.

earnings.name: Micron Technology
earnings.headline: Micron FQ3 — the AI-memory print of the quarter
earnings.thesis: Micron reports fiscal Q3 after the close on 24 June. With HBM sold out for several quarters and 2026 capacity already booked, the print is less about the quarter just gone and more about 2027 pricing and the guide. A hard, dated binary for anyone holding the name — the kind of event you express with a structure rather than naked stock.

pillars.label: Catalyst clarity
pillars.note: Hard dated binary — FQ3 after the close on 24 Jun (sourced).

pillars.label: Setup & positioning
pillars.note: Stock ~$546, +70% YTD and near a 52-week high — richly positioned into the print (sourced).

pillars.label: Risk / reward & pricing
pillars.note: Implied vol is rich; reward/risk is better defined with a structure than with naked stock (estimated).

pillars.label: House-view fit
pillars.note: Squarely the AI & Productivity / memory-supercycle house view (sourced).

earnings.watch: 2027 HBM pricing and the forward guide — not the quarter just reported.

facts.text: HBM sold out for several quarters; full-year 2026 HBM capacity booked under binding contracts
Stock ~$546, +70% YTD, trading near a 52-week high into the print
Consensus near $33.5B revenue and ~81% gross margin

earnings.name: Accenture
earnings.headline: Accenture FQ3 — the read on enterprise AI spend
earnings.thesis: Accenture reports fiscal Q3 before the open on 18 June. It is the cleanest single read-through on whether enterprises are actually converting AI interest into bookings and consulting spend — a tell for the whole 'AI adopters' leg of the house view, beyond the chip names.

pillars.label: Catalyst clarity
pillars.note: Dated report — FQ3 before the open on 18 Jun (sourced).

pillars.label: Setup & positioning
pillars.note: Services multiple has de-rated on AI-disruption fears; expectations reset (estimated).

pillars.label: Risk / reward & pricing
pillars.note: A reasonable risk/reward into a reset bar; income overwrite is attractive (estimated).

pillars.label: House-view fit
pillars.note: Direct read on the 'AI adopters / software & services' house view (sourced).

earnings.watch: GenAI bookings, new-bookings growth and any commentary on AI cannibalising legacy services.

facts.text: Accenture hosts its FQ3 fiscal-2026 call at 8:00am EDT on 18 June (before open)
Watched as the bellwether for enterprise AI / consulting demand

earnings.name: FedEx
earnings.headline: FedEx FQ4 — the global-trade bellwether
earnings.thesis: FedEx reports fiscal Q4 on 23 June. As a freight and global-trade bellwether it is a live read on the industrial cycle and tariff drag — useful well beyond the stock itself for any book carrying cyclicals or wanting to size the 'broadening beyond mega-caps' rotation.

pillars.label: Catalyst clarity
pillars.note: Dated FQ4 report on 23 Jun (sourced).

pillars.label: Setup & positioning
pillars.note: Cyclical, sentiment-sensitive into a mixed freight backdrop (estimated).

pillars.label: Risk / reward & pricing
pillars.note: Cheap multiple, but guidance risk is two-sided (estimated).

pillars.label: House-view fit
pillars.note: Supports the broadening / quality-cyclicals house view as a macro tell (estimated).

earnings.watch: FY27 guide, freight demand, DRIVE cost programme and any tariff/trade commentary.

facts.text: FedEx reports its next (FQ4) results on 23 June
Treated as a global-trade / industrial-cycle bellwether

earnings.name: Nike
earnings.headline: Nike FQ4 — a single-name turnaround bet
earnings.thesis: Nike reports fiscal Q4 after the close on 30 June. This is a company-specific turnaround story — inventory clean-up, China and the wholesale reset — rather than a desk theme. Lower conviction and off-theme, but a real catalyst for any book with consumer-discretionary exposure.
earnings.offThemeWhy: No standing desk theme on consumer discretionary — this is a single-name turnaround catalyst, judged on its own merit, not a strategic house view.

pillars.label: Catalyst clarity
pillars.note: Dated FQ4 after the close on 30 Jun (sourced).

pillars.label: Setup & positioning
pillars.note: Multi-quarter turnaround with uneven visibility; story-stock risk (estimated).

pillars.label: Risk / reward & pricing
pillars.note: Beaten-down expectations cut both ways; cash-secured puts pay you to wait (estimated).

pillars.label: House-view fit
pillars.note: Off-theme — no consumer-discretionary house view behind it (sourced).

earnings.watch: Gross-margin recovery, China trajectory and the FY27 revenue guide.

facts.text: Nike reports FQ4 fiscal-2026 results after the close on 30 June

exEarnings.name: Gold — accumulate the pullback
exEarnings.headline: Gold ~25% off the highs while central banks keep buying
exEarnings.thesis: Gold is trading around $4,165, roughly a quarter below its January record, even as central banks stay net buyers and every major bank's year-end target sits well above spot. For under-protected books this pullback is an accumulation window in the cleanest debasement hedge — sized to the protection gap, not chased.

pillars.label: Catalyst clarity
pillars.note: No single dated catalyst — a structural buy on a price pullback (sourced).

pillars.label: Setup & positioning
pillars.note: ~25% below the Jan all-time high; positioning has reset (sourced).

pillars.label: Risk / reward & pricing
pillars.note: Bank targets $5,200-6,000 sit 25-44% above spot; accumulate via accumulator/ETC (sourced).

pillars.label: House-view fit
pillars.note: Core to the Gold & Currency house view as strategic ballast (sourced).

macro.detail: ~25% below the 28-Jan record of $5,589; central banks net +244t in Q1 and +17t in April; China adding for 18 straight months.
macro.watch: CPI and the Fed path; central-bank demand as the marginal buyer.

facts.text: Gold ~$4,165, about 25% below the 28-Jan all-time high of $5,589
Central banks net buyers: +244t in Q1 2026, +17t in April; China 18 consecutive months
Bank year-end targets: Goldman $5,400, JPMorgan ~$6,000, Morgan Stanley $5,200, UBS $5,500

exEarnings.name: AI power & the grid
exEarnings.headline: Electricity is the binding constraint on AI
exEarnings.thesis: Datacenter electricity demand is on track for roughly 1,050 TWh in 2026 — doubling in a few years — with gas and nuclear the dispatchable supply. Owning the power and grid build-out is the lower-beta way to stay long the AI theme, and it pays contracted income while it compounds.

pillars.label: Catalyst clarity
pillars.note: Structural demand story rather than a single dated event (sourced).

pillars.label: Setup & positioning
pillars.note: Multi-year rate-base and load growth; still under-owned vs the chip names (estimated).

pillars.label: Risk / reward & pricing
pillars.note: Rate-sensitive, but contracted income cushions the entry (estimated).

pillars.label: House-view fit
pillars.note: Core Power & Infrastructure house view (sourced).

macro.detail: IEA projects datacenter electricity use approaching ~1,050 TWh by 2026; dispatchable gas and nuclear lead the supply, with SMRs entering after 2030.
macro.watch: Utility rate-base approvals, datacenter PPAs and nuclear/SMR newsflow.

facts.text: IEA: datacenter electricity use approaching ~1,050 TWh by 2026, more than double recent levels
Gas and nuclear are the dispatchable supply; SMRs enter the mix after 2030

exEarnings.name: Lock yields before the cuts
exEarnings.headline: The Fed holds — extend duration while you still can
exEarnings.thesis: The FOMC meets 16-17 June and is expected to hold at 3.50-3.75% under new chair Kevin Warsh, with cuts pushed later into 2026. That is the window to extend out of cash and bills into intermediate high-quality bonds — lock today's yields and add the ballast that cash can't give a book.

pillars.label: Catalyst clarity
pillars.note: Dated FOMC decision 16-17 Jun; a hold is the consensus (sourced).

pillars.label: Setup & positioning
pillars.note: Front-end yields still attractive but reinvestment risk is rising (sourced).

pillars.label: Risk / reward & pricing
pillars.note: Lock ~4.3-4.5% and add convexity if cuts land later in 2026 (estimated).

pillars.label: House-view fit
pillars.note: Core Fixed Income house view — extend duration before the cutting cycle (sourced).

macro.detail: Reuters poll consensus is a hold at the 16-17 June meeting — the first under chair Kevin Warsh — with sticky inflation pushing cuts later into 2026.
macro.watch: The dot plot / Warsh's tone, and any shift in the cut timeline.

facts.text: FOMC meets 16-17 June; consensus expects a hold at 3.50-3.75% (first meeting under chair Kevin Warsh)
Sticky inflation has pushed expected cuts later into 2026 (Reuters poll)

exEarnings.name: Energy — tactical on Hormuz risk
exEarnings.headline: Brent back to ~$87 as Hormuz de-escalates — a tactical, off-theme call
exEarnings.thesis: Brent has fallen back to about $87 from an April peak near $118 as US-Iran de-escalation reopened the Strait of Hormuz. With the geopolitical premium half-deflated, energy majors offer a tactical, income-paying hedge against a re-escalation — explicitly off the standing house view, a trade rather than a strategic tilt.
exEarnings.offThemeWhy: There is no standing desk theme on oil — this is a tactical, event-driven idea off Middle-East supply risk, sized as a hedge, not a strategic house view.

pillars.label: Catalyst clarity
pillars.note: Headline-driven — hinges on the Hormuz / Iran negotiation path (sourced).

pillars.label: Setup & positioning
pillars.note: Premium half-deflated from the April spike; two-sided (sourced).

pillars.label: Risk / reward & pricing
pillars.note: Majors pay a dividend while you hold the optionality; overwrite for income (estimated).

pillars.label: House-view fit
pillars.note: Off-theme tactical hedge, not a strategic tilt (sourced).

macro.detail: Down from an April peak of ~$118 as US-Iran de-escalation hopes reopened the Strait of Hormuz; still headline-sensitive.
macro.watch: The Hormuz / Iran negotiation path and OPEC+ supply signals.

facts.text: Brent ~$87 on 14 June, down from an April peak near $118
Move driven by US-Iran de-escalation hopes and a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz
